title: Zero-amount Bento deposits let arbitrary callers capture surplus tokens parked at BentoBox
reason: This appears to rely on BentoBox's own public skim/deposit-from-vault behavior (`deposit(..., from = address(bentoBox))`), so the router is not introducing a new privilege or theft path.

title: Missing slippage protection in swapUniV2
reason: The contract enforces a route-level `amountOutMin` at the end of `processRouteInternal`; slippage is not unchecked just because individual hops lack per-hop minimums.

title: No slippage protection for Trident pool swaps
reason: The final `amountOutMin` check applies to Trident routes as well, so this is not a separate missing-slippage bug.

title: No deadline/timestamp validation for route execution
reason: Missing deadlines are a user-experience/control limitation, not a standalone protocol exploit when the caller already chooses whether to submit the transaction and can set `amountOutMin`.

title: Unrestricted arbitrary recipient for BentoBox deposits
reason: Route-controlled recipients are intended behavior by themselves. The reportable issue is the missing binding between external `tokenIn` and the route-selected source asset, which is captured separately.

title: Missing reentrancy protection in swap callbacks
reason: The actionable callback flaw is the arbitrary-pool/authentication issue already kept. No additional exploitable reentrant path is demonstrated beyond that.

title: Unlimited token approvals for external tokens
reason: Incorrect premise: this contract does not grant token approvals in the cited code paths; it only uses transfers and transferFrom.

title: Division truncation leads to precision loss in share distribution
reason: This is bounded rounding dust from integer math and the share-based route format, not realistic protocol-level harm.

title: Missing validation of swapData for Trident pools
reason: `swapData` is intentionally pool-specific opaque calldata. Without an independent authorization failure, arbitrary data alone is not a router vulnerability.

title: Insufficient input validation on 'to' address
reason: A caller choosing a bad recipient is self-inflicted misuse, not a protocol bug.

title: Manipulable balance check using msg.sender
reason: The stated callback-based manipulation is speculative. The concrete balance-check failure is the internal-inventory and asset-mismatch issues already captured separately.

title: Slot undrain protection uses unchecked subtraction incorrectly
reason: Leaving dust when balance is 1 is an edge effect of the deliberate undrain pattern, not an exploitable vulnerability.

title: Silent failure possible in transferValueAndprocessRoute
reason: This concerns revert-message quality only and does not create realistic loss, theft, lockup, or DoS.

title: No validation that tokenOut is different from tokenIn
reason: Swapping a token into itself is a route-construction mistake, not an unauthorized or protocol-level exploit.

title: No event emissions for critical operations
reason: Lack of events is an observability issue, not a security vulnerability.
